message: Heads up — seeing elevated latency on the auth service. Token validation is taking 4-6s instead of the normal <200ms.
Users are getting logged out mid-session. Getting reports from customer success team — Salesforce integration is breaking because JWT refresh is failing.
Datadog shows Redis session cache in eu-west-1 at 92% memory — evicting active sessions. That's the lag.
Root cause: refresh token rotation feature deployed last night has a 24h TTL — doubling cache size over 12 hours. Emergency flush started.
Flush 60% done. Redis memory down to 74%. Auth latency dropping — p99 at 1.2s and falling. ETA to normal: 15-20 minutes.
Hotfix PR is ready — reduces rotation TTL from 24h to 1h. Gets us back to safe memory levels permanently.
Reviewed and approved the hotfix PR. Latency is back to 800ms and dropping. Users should stop seeing logouts very soon.
Flush complete. Redis at 41%. Auth latency p99 = 190ms — back to baseline. Hotfix deployed to eu-west-1. 🟢
Salesforce integration restored. Enterprise account team confirmed SSO is working again. All 3 affected accounts back online.
Incident resolved at 11:45 UTC. Duration: 31 minutes. No customer data impacted. Closing INC0043887. Great catch and fast fix — will add token rotation testing to our pre-deploy checklist.
